steps.title: Use the study's finding
steps.content: The opening Norway-Cameroon comparison led some people to speculate that democratic institutions weaken inflation control. Kapoor and colleagues' broader study found instead that democratic strength, when associated with inequality, restrains inflationary pressures.

steps.title: Reject overread comparisons
steps.content: Choice A overstates inequality while downplaying institutions, even though the study examines their interaction. Choice B invents faulty inflation measures. Choice D questions index accuracy, which the passage never does. The study shows that the two-country comparison should not be treated as proof of an inherent democratic shortcoming. The answer is C.
